seoTitle: Stokes Bay vs Stone Hut — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Stokes Bay and Stone Hut. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Stokes Bay vs Stone Hut: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Stokes Bay and Stone Hut.
heading: Stokes Bay vs Stone Hut — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Stokes Bay, South Australia and Stone Hut, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Stone Hut has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Stokes Bay (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Stokes Bay or Stone Hut safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Stokes Bay has a flood risk score of 47/100, while Stone Hut scores 35/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Stokes Bay scores 61/100 for bushfire risk. Stone Hut scores 34/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Stokes Bay or Stone Hut?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Stone Hut has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.98% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
